neighbors.name: Josue & Diana Flores
neighbors.claims: Josue and Diana Flores own a parcel of land in Cochise County, but they have no evident public profile or recorded involvement in local civic matters [Whitepages](https://www.whitepages.com/name/Josue-Flores/Arizona). No meeting minutes or news articles were found that mention this couple’s views on area development or community issues [Sierra Vista Herald](https://www.myheraldreview.com/search/?q=Josue+Flores).

approach_recommendations.engage: Since the Flores have not publicly voiced concerns, a friendly, personal introduction by the development team is advisable, listening to any questions they have about the project.

neighbors.name: Loren J. Sheldon
neighbors.claims: Loren J. Sheldon has operated a sand and gravel pit business on his Whetstone property for over 20 years [The Tombstone News](https://thetombstonenews.com/the-blame-game-continues-p682-80.htm). In 2006, he obtained a county rezoning to use his land for heavy industrial purposes, including storing and crushing automobiles under a Department of Homeland Security contract [The Tombstone News](https://thetombstonenews.com/flooding-causes-stink-in-whetstone-p646-1.htm). Sheldon’s property was also used as a temporary storage site for thousands of gallons of gasoline-contaminated soil after a tanker spill, a role that drew local scrutiny but demonstrated his cooperation with environmental cleanup efforts [The Tombstone News](https://thetombstonenews.com/flooding-causes-stink-in-whetstone-p646-1.htm).

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage Sheldon via a technical liaison (e.g., an engineer or county official) who can address infrastructure and drainage plans. Emphasizing how the project will manage storm runoff and respect his property’s operations can help build trust.

neighbors.name: Richard E. & Julia Ann Wilson
neighbors.claims: Richard E. and Julia Ann Wilson own a residential parcel in the same area of Cochise County [Cochise County Assessor](https://asr.cochise.az.gov/Production/RealWareDataMartPropertyInquiryWebsite/Inquiry.aspx). They do not appear in local news reports or public meeting records, suggesting they have maintained a low profile in community affairs [Herald/Review Media](https://www.myheraldreview.com/search/?q=Richard+Wilson+Cochise). No public statements or positions on development or energy projects by the Wilsons have been documented in available sources.

approach_recommendations.engage: The Wilsons have no evident public stance, so approach them in an informal, neighborly way. Ensure they have a direct point of contact for questions and emphasize that their quality of life will be respected.

neighbors.name: Donald Franz
neighbors.claims: Donald Franz is listed as a landowner on a Cochise County parcel near the proposed development site [Cochise County Treasurer](https://parcelinquirytreasurer.cochise.az.gov). No public records indicate that he has participated in local government meetings or community debates on development【75†】. There are no news articles or petitions found in which Franz has expressed any views regarding land development or renewable energy projects in the area.

approach_recommendations.engage: Franz has not taken a visible stance, so a straightforward, one-on-one outreach is recommended. Meeting him personally to explain the project and address any concerns (such as property access or maintenance) can help establish rapport.

overview_summary: Four neighboring landowners were reviewed, but none have a documented public stance on development. One (Loren Sheldon) is a well-known local business owner with notable community presence, while the others appear to have low public profiles. Influence is concentrated in Sheldon’s hands, as he is the only high-profile figure among these neighbors.
